key_insight: CaresAI predicts TNM pathology-stage labels from TCGA reports using text features and embeddings, with combined representations improving classification performance.
clinical_relevance: The approach could help automate cancer staging from pathology text, but it remains a research baseline rather than a deployable clinical tool.
method: Used TF-IDF plus ClinicalBERT, BioBERT, and PubMedBERT embeddings
Compared Logistic Regression, LightGBM, FFNN, and Wide Residual Networks
Framed T, N, and M as separate multi-label classification tasks
evidence_signal: Best training performance came from LightGBM with TF-IDF (AUROC 0.9368 T, 0.9524 N, 0.8311 M), while Codabench test Macro-F1 varied widely across sets, indicating unstable generalization.
watch_item: Performance dropped on evaluation and the authors note sensitivity to class imbalance and long clinical documents, so real-world use needs further validation.
paper_title: CaresAI at SMM4H-HeaRD 2026: Predicting TNM Staging
